mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous CPIAUCSL growth above 3.0 percent and FEDFUNDS below 2.0 percent creates a stagflationary pricing environment that compresses real corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral when both inflation exceeds 3.0 percent and policy rates stay below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index expansion above 102.0 during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 102.0 during late-cycle phases consistently precede negative 20-day SPX returns due to capacity constraints or demand destruction.

mechanism_narrative: Real sales of new homes exceeding 650,000 units indicates strong housing demand and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as financial and consumer sectors rally.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 70% of months where RSAFS surpasses 650,000 units.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase over a 3-month window confirms structural labor market deterioration, forcing equity valuations lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where payrolls fall and unemployment rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion that directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates above 2.0 percent show zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200,000 units signals collapsing construction activity and tightening credit conditions, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where HOUST drops below 1,200,000 units.
